title: Studies on Hydroxamic Acids. Part IV. Separation and Spectrophotometric Determination of lron(III)
author: Y. K. AGRAWAL
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Indian Institute of Technology, Powai, Bombay-400 076. 

  Manuscript received 31 July 1975, revised 20 February 1976 ; accepted 30 March 1976.  

 A new spectrophotometric method for the micro determination of iron(M) in the presence of commonly occurring metal ions using  \(p\) -nitrobenzohydroxamic acid ( \(p\) -NBHA) has been described. The reagent,  \(p\) -NBHA has the advantage that it furnishes a stable, water soluble complex which can be easily extracted into ethyl acetate. 

 The yellowish orange extract has a  \(\lambda\)   \(max\)   at 430 um and obeys Beer's law over the range 0.4-30 µg/ml of iron. 

 The extraction is quantitative at  \(p\) H 4.8-5.0. The studies on the effect of varying volumes as well as varying concentrations suggest the use of 10 ml of 0.005  M   \(p\) -NBHA for the extraction. The effect of diverse ions on the extraction behaviour has also been investigated.
